content:
# Matthew Chapter 12 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_012_page_001.jpg, MAT_012_page_002.jpg, MAT_012_page_003.jpg

1. In that season the Yeshua went on the Sabbaths through the grain-fields; and the disciples of him hungered and began to pluck heads [of grain] and to eat.

2. And the Pharisaioi, having seen, said to him, "Behold, the disciples of you do what is not lawful to do on [a] Sabbath."

3. And he said to them, "Have you not read what David did when he hungered, and those with him?

4. How he entered into the house of the God and the loaves of the presentation they ate, which it was not lawful for him to eat, nor for those with him, but only for the priests?

5. Or have you not read in the law that on the Sabbaths the priests in the temple profane the Sabbath and are guiltless?

6. But I say to you that [something] greater than the temple is here.

7. But if you had known what is, 'Mercy I desire and not sacrifice,' you would not have condemned the guiltless.

8. For lord is of the Sabbath the Son of the Man."

9. And having departed from there, he came into the synagogue of them.

10. And behold, a man having [a] withered hand. And they asked him, saying, "Is it lawful on the Sabbaths to heal?" — in order that they might accuse him.

11. And he said to them, "What man will there be among you who will have one sheep, and if this falls on the Sabbaths into a pit, will he not take hold of it and lift [it] out?

12. How much more, then, is a man worth than a sheep! So it is lawful on the Sabbaths to do good."

13. Then he says to the man, "Stretch out the hand of you." And he stretched [it] out, and it was restored, sound as the other.

14. And having gone out, the Pharisaioi took counsel against him, how they might destroy him.

15. And the Yeshua, having known, withdrew from there. And many followed him, and he healed them all,

16. and he warned them that they should not make him known,

17. in order that what was spoken through Esaias the prophet might be fulfilled, saying,

18. "Behold, the servant of me whom I chose, the beloved of me in whom the soul of me is well-pleased. I will put the Spirit of me upon him, and judgment to the nations he will proclaim.

19. He will not quarrel nor cry out, nor will anyone hear the voice of him in the streets.

20. A reed having been bruised he will not break, and a wick smoking he will not quench, until he puts forth the judgment to victory.

21. And in the name of him [the] nations will hope."

22. Then was brought to him a [man] being demonized, blind and mute; and he healed him, so that the mute [man] spoke and saw.

23. And all the crowds were amazed and were saying, "Is this the Son of David?"

24. But the Pharisaioi, having heard, said, "This [man] does not cast out the demons except by Beelzeboul, the ruler of the demons."

25. And having known the thoughts of them, he said to them, "Every kingdom having been divided against itself is brought-to-desolation, and every city or house having been divided against itself will not stand.

26. And if the Satanas casts out the Satanas, he was divided against himself; how then will the kingdom of him stand?

27. And if I by Beelzeboul cast out the demons, the sons of you by whom do they cast out? Therefore they will be judges of you.

28. But if by [the] Spirit of God I cast out the demons, then has come upon you the kingdom of the God.

29. Or how is anyone able to enter into the house of the strong [man] and the goods of him to plunder, unless first he binds the strong [man]? And then the house of him he will plunder.

30. The [one] not being with me is against me, and the [one] not gathering with me scatters.

31. Therefore I say to you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven to men, but the blasphemy of the Spirit will not be forgiven.

32. And whoever speaks a word against the Son of the Man, it will be forgiven to him; but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit, it will not be forgiven to him, neither in this age nor in the coming [one].

33. Either make the tree good and the fruit of it good, or make the tree rotten and the fruit of it rotten; for from the fruit the tree is known.

34. Offspring of vipers, how are you able to speak good [things], being evil? For out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaks.

35. The good man out of the good treasure brings forth good [things], and the evil man out of the evil treasure brings forth evil [things].

36. But I say to you that every idle word which men will speak, they will give account concerning it in [the] day of judgment.

37. For by the words of you you will be justified, and by the words of you you will be condemned."

38. Then answered him some of the scribes and Pharisaioi, saying, "Teacher, we wish to see a sign from you."

39. And answering, he said to them, "An evil and adulterous generation seeks a sign, and a sign will not be given to it except the sign of Ionas the prophet.

40. For just as Ionas was in the belly of the sea-monster three days and three nights, so will the Son of the Man be in the heart of the earth three days and three nights.

41. Men of Nineveh will stand up in the judgment with this generation and will condemn it, for they repented at the proclamation of Ionas, and behold, [something] greater than Ionas [is] here.

42. [The] queen of [the] south will rise in the judgment with this generation and will condemn it, for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and behold, [something] greater than Solomon [is] here.

43. But when the unclean spirit goes out from the man, it passes through waterless places seeking rest, and does not find [it].

44. Then it says, 'I will return to the house of me from where I came out.' And having come, it finds [it] standing empty, having been swept and having been put-in-order.

45. Then it goes and takes along with itself seven other spirits more evil than itself, and having entered they dwell there; and the last [things] of the man that become worse than the first. So it will be also to this evil generation."

46. While he was still speaking to the crowds, behold, the mother of him and the brothers of him stood outside, seeking to speak to him.

47. And someone said to him, "Behold, the mother of you and the brothers of you outside stand, seeking to speak to you."

48. And answering, he said to the [one] telling him, "Who is the mother of me, and who are the brothers of me?"

49. And having stretched out the hand of him over the disciples of him, he said, "Behold, the mother of me and the brothers of me.

50. For whoever does the will of the Father of me, the [one] in [the] heavens, he is the brother of me and sister and mother."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **μεῖζον (vv.6, 41, 42):** "Greater" — neuter form, meaning "something greater," not "someone greater." Jesus does not say "I am greater than the temple/Jonah/Solomon" directly, but that something greater is present — the kingdom itself.

2. **Βεελζεβούλ (v.24):** The full Beelzebul controversy, anticipated in 9:34 and 10:25. The Pharisees' accusation that Jesus operates by demonic power triggers his discourse on the unforgivable sin.

3. **v.28 — "Spirit of God":** Sinaiticus reads "Spirit of God" (πνεύματι θεοῦ). Luke's parallel (11:20) reads "finger of God" (δακτύλῳ θεοῦ). Matthew's version makes the pneumatological point explicit.

4. **v.32 — Blasphemy against the Spirit:** The most severe warning in the Gospels. Speaking against the Son of Man (misunderstanding Jesus' identity) is forgivable; attributing the Spirit's work to Satan is not — "neither in this age nor in the coming one."

5. **v.40 — κήτους:** "Sea-monster" — the Greek κῆτος (cf. "cetacean") is a large sea creature, not specifically a "whale." It echoes the LXX of Jonah 2:1.

— EMET, AI Translator
